calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-06-2026 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-06-2026 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, NA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank N.A.'s unopposed motion for summary judgment is granted.

A party may move for summary adjudication in an action or proceeding if it is contended that the cause of action has no merit. (Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 437c(a)(1).) The motion for summary adjudication shall be granted if all the papers submitted show that there is no triable issue as to any material fact. (Id., Sec. 437c(c).) The moving party must make an initial showing, based upon affirmative evidence, that summary adjudication must be granted. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Company (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) If the movant meets this initial burden, the burden then shifts to the opposing party to show that a triable issue of one or more material facts exists. The court liberally construes the evidence in support of the party opposing summary adjudication and resolves doubts concerning the evidence in favor of that party. (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1039.) The moving party bears the burden of persuasion "from the commencement to conclusion" to show "that there is no triable issue of material fact and that [it] is entitled to [summary adjudication] as a matter of law." (Id.)

Here, Wells Fargo has produced evidence sufficient to show it is entitled to judgment on its breach of contract cause of action and its common counts / money lent cause of action. The Smith Declaration establishes that Wells Fargo and defendant Ai L. Feng entered into the credit card contract (Smith Decl., paras. 9-15), Feng used the credit card (id., para. 15), and Feng stopped making payments when a balance of $16,061.60 remained on the account (id., paras. 21-22 & pdf p. 799). This evidence meets Wells Fargo's burden of showing that it is entitled to judgment. Feng has not produced evidence to create a triable issue of fact on any element of Wells Fargo's causes of action. The motion is granted. Wells Fargo shall submit a proposed judgment.

Wells Fargo has submitted a cost memorandum in advance of the judgment. The memorandum will be deemed filed for purposes of Feng's motion to strike after service of the notice of entry of judgment. (Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 3.1700, subd. (a)(1).)
Counsel for the prevailing party is required to deliver to Dept. 301 a paper copy of the proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
